api: Brex Platform REST API (https://platform.brexapis.com, documented at https://api.brex.com per developer.brex.com)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the live published per-product OpenAPI bundles (developer.brex.com/_bundle/openapi/{transactions,expenses,team,payments,webhooks,budgets,accounting,fields,onboarding,travel}_api.yaml, 108 method+path operations across 10 API products, fetched 2026-07-04): 16 read streams (transactions, users, expenses, vendors, budgets, departments, locations, titles, legal_entities, cards, accounts_card, accounts_cash, card_statements, linked_accounts, transfers, webhooks) and 14 write actions covering vendor/department/location/title/user/card/expense/webhook lifecycle mutations. Excluded: EVERY write endpoint whose OpenAPI definition marks the Idempotency-Key request header required:true (create_vendor, create_transfer, create_incoming_transfer, create_card, create/update budget and spend_limit at both v1 and v2, create_webhook, create_webhook_group) as requires_elevated_scope ENGINE_GAP entries — engine.WriteAction has no headers field and write.go's executeWriteRecord calls Requester.Do/DoForm with an unconditional nil headers argument, so this dialect cannot attach ANY header, let alone a fresh-per-request idempotency key, to a write request; the surrounding update/delete/lock/unlock/terminate actions on the same resources ARE modeled since Brex marks Idempotency-Key optional on those. Also excluded: the entire Travel API (a distinct product, no consuming stream), Accounting API (Brex's own outbound accounting-sync integration surface, not card/expense/vendor data), Fields API (custom-field schema/value management, account-schema-adjacent), Onboarding/referrals API (partner-onboarding, unrelated to financial-data sync), webhook groups (a partner multi-tenant fan-out feature this single-account connector does not use), the deprecated v1 Budgets surface (superseded by the v2 budgets stream already covered), and v2 spend_limits (Brex models spend limits as budgets with limit_type=budget — same underlying record shape the budgets stream already reads) as out_of_scope/deprecated/duplicate_of breadth-vs-cost triage; single-object detail endpoints duplicating an already-covered list stream, PCI-scoped card-PAN/webhook-signing-secret reads, and non-data endpoints (company/current-user identity snapshots) round out the exclusion set.

excluded.reason: single primary-cash-account detail; identical record shape to the accounts_cash list stream's per-item object (primary=true row), already covered by that stream
single cash-account detail by id; identical record shape to the accounts_cash list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

cash-account statements scoped by id; same record shape as card_statements, and this connector's primary account is the card account, not cash — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, no consuming stream added for the cash variant this pass

cash-account transaction list scoped by account id; this connector's existing transactions stream targets the primary CARD account only (legacy parity), Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage for the cash-account variant

all-expense-types list (card + reimbursement + travel); the expenses stream already covers card expenses, this connector's original scope, and the unfiltered variant returns a mixed-type union this dialect's single schema cannot cleanly project without losing type-specific fields
attempts to match an uploaded receipt to an existing expense; a matching/reconciliation action, not a plain record mutation
single card-expense detail; identical record shape to the expenses list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
uploads a binary receipt image/PDF (multipart), not a plain JSON/form data mutation this dialect can express
generic (type-unaware) single-expense detail; same underlying record as GET /v1/expenses/card/{expense_id} for card expenses, already covered by the expenses stream

creating a new physical/virtual card requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) that this engine's declarative write dialect has no field to attach on ANY write request (engine.WriteAction has no headers field; write.go's executeWriteRecord calls Requester.Do/DoForm with a nil headers argument unconditionally) — a genuine ENGINE_GAP, not a scoping choice; update_card/lock_card/unlock_card/terminate_card cover the rest of the card lifecycle since their Idempotency-Key is optional on Brex's own spec
single-card detail; identical record shape to the cards list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns the card's full unmasked PAN/CVV; PCI-scoped sensitive-data endpoint gated behind elevated card-data entitlement, never modeled as ordinary sync data
emails a secure link to view the card number; a notification/delivery action, not a data mutation
single company-identity snapshot for the authenticated account, not a syncable object list
single-department detail; identical record shape to the departments list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-legal-entity detail; identical record shape to the legal_entities list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-location detail; identical record shape to the locations list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-title detail; identical record shape to the titles list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns the identity of the currently-authenticated user/token, not a syncable object stream
single-user detail; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
a user's card-spend limit sub-resource; already embedded on the users/cards records' own limit-adjacent fields, not a distinct list-shaped resource
sets a per-user card-spend limit; a narrow admin-console action, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

creating an incoming transfer requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) this engine's declarative write dialect cannot attach to any write request — a genuine ENGINE_GAP (see /v2/cards POST's identical reasoning), not a scoping choice

creating a money transfer requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) this engine's declarative write dialect cannot attach to any write request — a genuine ENGINE_GAP (see /v2/cards POST's identical reasoning), not a scoping choice; the highest-consequence write in the entire Brex surface, doubly appropriate to block rather than approximate
single-transfer detail; identical record shape to the transfers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creating a vendor requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) this engine's declarative write dialect cannot attach to any write request — a genuine ENGINE_GAP (see /v2/cards POST's identical reasoning), not a scoping choice; update_vendor/delete_vendor cover the rest of the vendor lifecycle since their Idempotency-Key is optional on Brex's own spec
single-vendor detail; identical record shape to the vendors list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creating a webhook subscription requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) this engine's declarative write dialect cannot attach to any write request — a genuine ENGINE_GAP (see /v2/cards POST's identical reasoning), not a scoping choice; update_webhook/delete_webhook cover the rest of the webhook lifecycle since neither requires the header
webhook-group (partner multi-tenant fan-out) list; a partner-tier feature this connector's single-account scope does not use, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

creating a webhook group requires a mandatory Idempotency-Key request header (Brex OpenAPI: required:true) this engine's declarative write dialect cannot attach to any write request — a genuine ENGINE_GAP; also a partner-tier feature this connector's single-account scope does not use

removes member accounts from a webhook group; partner-tier feature this connector's single-account scope does not use
